  1. Architecture
  2. Gemini API Client

Gemini API Client

Type System

The TypeScript types mirror the macOS Swift Codable types exactly:

interface TextPart { text: string; }
interface InlineDataPart {
  inlineData: { mimeType: string; data: string; };
}
type Part = TextPart | InlineDataPart;

interface Content { parts: Part[]; role?: string; }
interface GenerateContentRequest {
  contents: Content[];
  generationConfig?: GenerationConfig;
}
interface GenerateContentResponse {
  candidates: Candidate[];
}

GeminiClient Class

A simple class wrapping the fetch API:

export class GeminiClient {
  private readonly apiKey: string;

  static buildApiUrl(apiKey: string): string {
    return `${GEMINI_BASE_URL}/${GEMINI_MODEL_ID}:generateContent?key=${apiKey}`;
  }

  async generateImage(prompt: string): Promise<GenerateImageResult> {
    const url = GeminiClient.buildApiUrl(this.apiKey);
    const request = buildGenerateRequest(prompt, {
      responseMimeType: "image/png",
      responseModalities: ["Text", "Image"],
    });

    const response = await fetch(url, {
      method: "POST",
      headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" },
      body: JSON.stringify(request),
    });

    if (!response.ok) {
      const errorBody = await response.json();
      throw new GeminiAPIError(
        errorBody.error.message,
        errorBody.error.status,
        response.status,
      );
    }

    const data = await response.json();
    const image = parseImageFromResponse(data);
    if (!image) throw new Error("No image data in response");

    return { imageData: image.data, mimeType: image.mimeType };
  }
}

Error Handling

The GeminiAPIError class includes a isProhibitedContent getter for handling content policy violations:

export class GeminiAPIError extends Error {
  get isProhibitedContent(): boolean {
    return this.status === "PROHIBITED_CONTENT";
  }
}
WarningPROHIBITED_CONTENT

The Gemini API may reject prompts that reference copyrighted characters. Our Yoda prompt uses only physical traits (green skin, pointed ears, brown robes) without any IP-specific names.

MemeService

The orchestrator that ties API calls to file storage:

export async function generateAndSaveMeme(
  apiKey: string,
  prompt: string,
  overlayText: string,
): Promise<MemeResult> {
  const client = new GeminiClient(apiKey);

  // Include overlay text in AI prompt
  const fullPrompt = overlayText
    ? `${prompt}\n\nRender the following text prominently...`
    : prompt;

  const result = await client.generateImage(fullPrompt);

  // Save base64 image to local filesystem
  const file = new File(Paths.document, `meme_${Date.now()}.png`);
  const bytes = new Uint8Array(/* base64 decode */);
  file.write(bytes);

  return { imageUri: file.uri, mimeType: result.mimeType };
}
NoteExpo SDK 55 File API

Expo SDK 55 replaced documentDirectory and writeAsStringAsync with the new File / Directory / Paths class-based API. We use new File(Paths.document, filename) and file.write(bytes) for the modern pattern.

Mobile Architecture
State Management
Source Code
---
title: "Gemini API Client"
---

## Type System

The TypeScript types mirror the macOS Swift `Codable` types exactly:

```typescript
interface TextPart { text: string; }
interface InlineDataPart {
  inlineData: { mimeType: string; data: string; };
}
type Part = TextPart | InlineDataPart;

interface Content { parts: Part[]; role?: string; }
interface GenerateContentRequest {
  contents: Content[];
  generationConfig?: GenerationConfig;
}
interface GenerateContentResponse {
  candidates: Candidate[];
}
```

## GeminiClient Class

A simple class wrapping the `fetch` API:

```typescript
export class GeminiClient {
  private readonly apiKey: string;

  static buildApiUrl(apiKey: string): string {
    return `${GEMINI_BASE_URL}/${GEMINI_MODEL_ID}:generateContent?key=${apiKey}`;
  }

  async generateImage(prompt: string): Promise<GenerateImageResult> {
    const url = GeminiClient.buildApiUrl(this.apiKey);
    const request = buildGenerateRequest(prompt, {
      responseMimeType: "image/png",
      responseModalities: ["Text", "Image"],
    });

    const response = await fetch(url, {
      method: "POST",
      headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" },
      body: JSON.stringify(request),
    });

    if (!response.ok) {
      const errorBody = await response.json();
      throw new GeminiAPIError(
        errorBody.error.message,
        errorBody.error.status,
        response.status,
      );
    }

    const data = await response.json();
    const image = parseImageFromResponse(data);
    if (!image) throw new Error("No image data in response");

    return { imageData: image.data, mimeType: image.mimeType };
  }
}
```

## Error Handling

The `GeminiAPIError` class includes a `isProhibitedContent` getter for handling content policy violations:

```typescript
export class GeminiAPIError extends Error {
  get isProhibitedContent(): boolean {
    return this.status === "PROHIBITED_CONTENT";
  }
}
```

::: {.callout-warning}
## PROHIBITED_CONTENT
The Gemini API may reject prompts that reference copyrighted characters. Our Yoda prompt uses only physical traits (green skin, pointed ears, brown robes) without any IP-specific names.
:::

## MemeService

The orchestrator that ties API calls to file storage:

```typescript
export async function generateAndSaveMeme(
  apiKey: string,
  prompt: string,
  overlayText: string,
): Promise<MemeResult> {
  const client = new GeminiClient(apiKey);

  // Include overlay text in AI prompt
  const fullPrompt = overlayText
    ? `${prompt}\n\nRender the following text prominently...`
    : prompt;

  const result = await client.generateImage(fullPrompt);

  // Save base64 image to local filesystem
  const file = new File(Paths.document, `meme_${Date.now()}.png`);
  const bytes = new Uint8Array(/* base64 decode */);
  file.write(bytes);

  return { imageUri: file.uri, mimeType: result.mimeType };
}
```

::: {.callout-note}
## Expo SDK 55 File API
Expo SDK 55 replaced `documentDirectory` and `writeAsStringAsync` with the new `File` / `Directory` / `Paths` class-based API. We use `new File(Paths.document, filename)` and `file.write(bytes)` for the modern pattern.
:::

NanoMeme Compendium
